question: Explain the simplified diagram of the Reactor Safety Valve (RSV).

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their protective cladding, and compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with NRC regulations and ASME code requirements for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details how the plant uses specific ASME BPVC and OM code cases for design, fabrication, inspection, and testing, ensuring all selected code cases meet the conditions established in relevant NRC regulatory guides. A consolidated list of these code cases is provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, ensuring effective heat removal and containment of radionuclides.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

edges.description: Section A provides detailed descriptions of specific pressurizer components and their materials, which are part of the broader reactor coolant system and pressurizer overview described in Section B.

Section A provides detailed descriptions of the pressurizer's components and materials, which are part of the reactor coolant system relevant to the overpressure protection requirements and code compliance discussed in Section B.

Section A provides detailed descriptions of the pressurizer's structural components and materials, which are part of the Reactor Coolant System described in Section B, thereby specifying the physical elements that enable the system functions outlined in Section B.
Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are detailed in Section B.

Section A describes the general design and components of the reactor coolant system, which includes the reactor pressure vessel and integral pressurizer, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that the design and construction of these components, including safety valves, must comply with.
Section B provides specific details about the reactor coolant system's degasification and venting functions, which are part of the general system described in Section A.

Section A provides a summary description of the reactor coolant system design, while Section B lists references that support the technical and regulatory basis for the integrity and quality assurance of the reactor coolant boundary, which includes components like the Reactor Safety Valve.

Section A outlines the regulatory and code compliance requirements for overpressure protection devices like the Reactor Safety Valve, while Section B describes the system features (such as vent valves and pressure-retaining components) that implement these requirements in the reactor design.

Section A discusses compliance with ASME and NRC regulatory codes for overpressure protection, while Section B lists specific references (such as ASME and EPRI documents) that support or are cited in Section A's compliance statements.

Section A describes the system functions and components relevant to reactor safety valves, while Section B lists reference documents that provide standards and guidelines supporting the integrity and quality assurance of those components.
